specific_mechanism: WNSP: Wasserstein Null-Space Projection. Maintain a 1-Lipschitz critic f_φ trained on samples from π_ref (pre-training reference policy). Each DPO/PPO gradient ∇_θ J is split into a component aligned with ∇_θ E_{π_θ}[f_φ] (the W1-active direction) and an orthogonal complement (W1-null). Updates are restricted to the null component when the W1 distance to π_ref exceeds budget B.
llm_application: WNSP (Wasserstein Null-Space Projection) RLHF rule: train a 1-Lipschitz critic f_φ on π_ref samples via WGAN-GP loss. At each preference-update step compute ∇_θ J_DPO and g_W = ∇_θ E_{π_θ}[f_φ]. The W1-null projection is ∇_θ J_⊥ = ∇_θ J - (⟨∇_θ J, g_W⟩/‖g_W‖²) g_W. When W1(π_θ, π_ref) > B, the update uses ∇_θ J_⊥ instead of ∇_θ J. Critic f_φ is refreshed every K steps.
novelty_claim: Wasserstein Policy Regularization (arXiv 2602.01685) ADDS W1 as a penalty; this candidate uses W1 dual gradient as a PROJECTOR onto the null space, restricting updates to W1-invariant directions when budget breaches.
motivation_justification: Kantorovich-Rubinstein dual IS a specific functional-analytic operator with the property that orthogonal directions to ∇f* are W1-invariant. The projection is a direct transfer of this operator, not analogy.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: 1-Lipschitz critic f_φ trained on reference distribution
M_2: Wasserstein-active gradient direction g_W = ∇_θ E[f_φ]
